query: Has Russia announced any extension of its gasoline (motor gasoline/benzine) export ban beyond July 31, 2026? Look for any cabinet resolution, decree, or official statement from late June 2026 or early July 2026 reported by TASS, Reuters, or government.ru. Also describe the recent status of Ukrainian drone strikes on Russian refineries and domestic gasoline shortages as of late June 2026, and historically how many days before each deadline past gasoline export ban extensions were formally announced.
answer:
I've completed the research. Here is a summary of the key findings:

## Summary

**1. Extension beyond July 31, 2026 — NOT announced.** As of June 30, 2026, no cabinet resolution, decree, or official statement extending Russia's gasoline export ban beyond July 31, 2026 has been published by TASS, Reuters, or government.ru. The current full ban (including producers) was set by the April 2, 2026 decree (government.ru/news/58316) and remains in force through July 31.

**Important nuance:** The late-June 2026 government discussion is focused on a possible **diesel** producer-export ban — not gasoline. Novak (June 23–26) said the diesel market has a *surplus* and a few-months ban is being weighed; Bloomberg reported (~June 28) Russia held off to reassess. No gasoline-ban extension was signaled.

**2. Refinery strikes & shortages (late June 2026):** Ukrainian drones have struck nearly every major Russian refinery (Meduza, June 29). Late-June hits include Slavyansk-na-Kubani (~June 28), Yaroslavl region (June 27–28), and Bashneft-Ufaneftekhim/Ufa (~June 25). On June 29, **Putin publicly admitted fuel shortages and gas-station queues** for the first time. Rosneft capped purchases at **50 liters/vehicle/day** in Irkutsk; Crimea suspended civilian gasoline sales (worst crisis since 2014).

**3. Historical lead time:** Past gasoline-ban extensions are formalized only **~1–5 days before the deadline**, typically after a Novak "signal" 1–2 weeks earlier. The end-2025 extension was signed **Dec 27, 2025 — ~4 days before** the Dec 31 expiry; the Dec-2025 extension was signaled Sep 25 and decreed Sep 30 (days before end-September). This means the absence of a July-2026 extension decree on June 30 (31 days out) is **fully consistent with the historical pattern** — any decision would typically land in the last week of July.

Full structured findings (key facts, sources, and quantitative data points) have been submitted via the StructuredOutput tool.

sources.title: Ban on gasoline exports from Russia extended to producers of petroleum products
sources.snippet: The Russian government has extended gasoline export restrictions effective until July 31, 2026, to direct producers of petroleum products.

sources.title: Russia Bans Producers From Exporting Gasoline Until July 31
sources.snippet: A new decree (government.ru 58316) extends the ban to producers; non-producers were barred until July 31.

sources.title: Russian government considers diesel fuel exports ban (Novak, June 23)
sources.snippet: Situation challenging but controlled; cabinet weighing diesel export ban. No mention of gasoline ban extension.

sources.title: Russian authorities considering ban on diesel exports by producers for several months
sources.snippet: Novak: short-term diesel ban of a few months considered; Russia has diesel surplus. Aviation kerosene ban runs to Nov 30, 2026.

sources.title: Russian deputy PM Novak to hold meeting on fuel market, diesel export ban to be discussed
sources.snippet: June 26 headquarters meeting on fuel market; possibility of banning diesel exports for producers.

sources.title: Putin says Russia faces fuel shortages after Ukraine strikes
sources.snippet: Putin conceded fuel shortages following long-range Ukrainian drone strikes on energy infrastructure.

sources.title: Ukraine's drone attacks hit more Russian refineries and create fuel shortages in Siberia
sources.snippet: Slavyansk-na-Kubani and Yaroslavl strikes; Rosneft limits Irkutsk purchases to 50 L/day; Crimea suspends civilian sales.

sources.title: Ukrainian drones have struck nearly every major Russian refinery
sources.snippet: Inventory of struck refineries incl. Bashneft-Ufaneftekhim (~June 25).

sources.title: Russia Holds Off on Diesel Export Ban to Reassess Monday
sources.snippet: Russia holds off on diesel export ban to reassess; Putin acknowledges fuel supply problems.

sources.title: Russia Extends Temporary Gasoline-Export Ban Through February
sources.snippet: Dec 27, 2025 extension of gasoline export ban to Feb 28, 2026.

sources.title: Russia Extends Ban on Gasoline Exports Until February
sources.snippet: New decree extended ban on gasoline exports until February 28, 2026 inclusive, all exporters incl. producers.

sources.title: Russia Extends Gasoline Export Ban, Restricts Diesel Shipments
sources.snippet: Sep 30, 2025 decree (government.ru 56377) extends gasoline ban to Dec 31; Novak signaled Sep 25.

sources.title: Russia Bans Jet Fuel Exports Until Late November
sources.snippet: June 1, 2026 jet fuel export ban until end Nov (government.ru 58885).

sources.title: Russia Is Grappling With Its Worst Nationwide Fuel Shortages In Years
sources.snippet: Worst nationwide fuel shortages in years following refinery strikes.

sources.title: Putin Acknowledges Russia Faces Fuel Supply Issues
sources.snippet: Putin: problems for motorists and businesses persist, queues at gas stations.
